Cardiovascular

Resting HR daily readings + 7-day rolling avg · 30d

Daily 7-day avg Walking avg (watch)
Daily RHR is noisy (sleep, hydration, alcohol can swing it ±5 bpm). The rolling average is the trend that matters; downward = improving cardiovascular efficiency.

Blood pressure sys / dia · 30d

Target: <120 / <80 (AHA normal). Both tracking in-range.

HRV ms · overnight · recovery quality

Daily 7-day avg ±1σ baseline band
Higher = better recovery. Dots outside the band are meaningfully off-baseline; trust the 7-day line over any single dip.

Activity

Daily steps 30d · target 10,000

Hevy workouts 30d · by focus

FocusSessionsTotal timeTotal setsVolume (lb)
Legs711h 22m176196,939
Core65h 11m289,195
Back68h 23m16997,549
Chest57h 08m14254,724
Other55h 14m6611,732
Grouped by primary muscle focus (the highest-volume muscle group per session).

Energy & Vitals Apple Watch · 30d

Energy balance intake vs measured burn · kcal

Basal burn Active burn Intake (logged)
14d avg: burn 3,050 kcal (Apple TDEE) vs intake 2,321 → deficit 729 kcal/day ≈ 1.5 lb/wk (14 days with both logged)

Respiratory rate breaths/min · nightly

Stable is good. A sustained rise of 1+ breaths/min over your baseline is an early illness / overtraining signal — trust the trend, not single nights.

Blood oxygen SpO₂ % · daily avg & min

Daily average should sit ≥95%. Occasional min-dips are normal (sensor placement, sleep position); repeated lows are worth mentioning to your care team.

Cardio fitness VO₂ Max · mL/kg/min

Wrist temperature nightly deviation

Recovery Signals daily composite

!
Jun 14: 48 / 100 — below baseline
all signals near baseline · 7d avg flat vs prior week · 4/4 signals.
Composite of sleep score, HRV, resting HR, and nightly respiratory rate z-scores (plus wrist temperature when exported) — each normalized against your own 30-day baseline, so the average trends toward 50 by design and the chart shows relative recovery swings. Hover any bar to see which signal is driving it. Faded bars had fewer than 2 signals that day — treat them as low-confidence.
≥ 70 — strong recovery 50–69 — average < 50 — below your own baseline

Key Markers

Hormone Panel

Testosterone — Total & Free ng/dL · over time

Total T (ng/dL) Free T (ng/dL ×10) Optimal range

Estradiol (E2) & SHBG pg/mL · ng/dL

Estradiol (pg/mL) SHBG (nmol/L)
E2 rises with TRT — watch for elevation above 40 pg/mL. SHBG affects free T availability.

Cardiovascular & Metabolic

Lipid panel mg/dL

Total Chol LDL HDL Trig

Glucose & HbA1c mg/dL · %

Fasting glucose HbA1c ×10

Hematocrit & Hemoglobin % · g/dL ×3

TRT raises hematocrit. Flag if >54% — may require therapeutic phlebotomy.
